channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Arrest made in Antioch greenway shooting in less than 24 hours
item.description: Investigative work by Homicide & Specialized Investigations detectives led to the arrest of Paul Park, 29, of Brentwood.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris and Trump campaigns pivot to turnout as early voting begins
item.description: With just 21 days to go before the final votes are cast in the 2024 presidential season, Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are scrambling to win over and turn out Black voters, women and other key constituencies in what looks to be a razor-tight election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: People with felony records can vote in Nebraska. Ruling could help tip the balance on Nov. 5
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Nebraska’s top election official had no authority to strip voting rights from people convicted of a felony, the state Supreme Court ruled Wednesday in a decision that could add hundreds of new voters to the rolls and potentially help tip the balance on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: Ride-hailing service Lyft announced that it will offer discounted rideshare, bike-share and scooters on Election Day, an initiative designed to increase transportation access for voters, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Entertainment and lifestyle titan Barry Diller will have memoir out next spring
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Barry Diller has a memoir coming out next spring, covering a life that could fill multiple volumes.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is headlining a rally in Atlanta alongside celebrities and local civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans challenge more than 63,000 voters in Georgia, but few removed, AP finds
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — From Georgia's mountains to its Atlantic shore, challenges to the qualifications of voters have rolled in this summer and fall, part of a wide-ranging national effort coordinated by Donald Trump’s allies to enlist Republican activists to remove people they view as suspect from the vot
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until Congress approves new funds
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson assured there would be support to provide necessary funds – when Congress returns after the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Arizona man who admitted to murdering his wife receives his sentence
item.description: Daniel Paduchowski was sentenced to 16 years in prison after admitting to the murder of his wife, Kelly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's prime minister is criticized for buying a waterfront home during a housing crisis
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has been criticized for buying a multimillion-dollar waterfront home during a national housing crisis with federal elections just months away.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecutor to 'review' Marilyn Manson rape allegations after victim calls DA out weeks before election
item.description: Los Angeles investigators have "new evidence" in connection with rape allegations against Marilyn Manson from former "Game of Thrones" actress Esme Bianco.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas man facing execution in shaken baby syndrome case awaits clemency ruling
item.description: A Texas man who this week could be the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome is awaiting a decision on his request for clemency from a state board.
item.dc:creator: Juan A. Lozano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Gun-wielding suspect gets beaten up by employees at pizzeria he tried to rob: police
item.description: A 19-year-old suspect in Texas was arrested after he allegedly tried to rob a Marco's Pizza location in Austin. Employees at the restaurant beat him up after he showed a gun.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon announced Tuesday that more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Family members say that he briefly left the venue to retrieve something and was followed by a man who accused him of cutting him off on the road when he came back.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, WGN 9

item.title: Former owner of dog found chained to pole ahead of Hurricane Milton arrested, to 'face the music,' DA says
item.description: The owner of a dog found chained to a pole during a hurricane has been identified and could face animal cruelty charges, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LSUS partners with Indian institute for dual graduate degree program
item.description: LSU Shreveport chancellor Robert Smith said the campus looks forward to welcoming Indian students into the program.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Tripp, NewsNation

item.title: Can Apple Watch 10 be used as a medical device?
item.description: It's increasingly clear that the future of the Apple Watch isn’t just as an alternative to cell phones.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, ABC 4

item.title: Florida armed robbery suspects' arrest caught on camera after victim tracks stolen phone: sheriff's office
item.description: Florida law enforcement officials arrested two men after they allegedly robbed a couple of victims and fled with their cellphones, which one victim was able to track down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA resumes door-to-door visits in North Carolina after threats tied to disinformation
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Federal disaster personnel have resumed door-to-door visits as part of their hurricane-recovery work in North Carolina, an effort temporarily suspended amid threats that prompted officials to condemn the spread of disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man allegedly killed by son,19, in California was renowned photojournalist
item.description: In a bizarre twist to a grisly crime on a popular hiking trail in the San Gabriel Mountains where a 19-year-old man allegedly stabbed his father to death, the victim has been identified as a well-known, award-winning war photographer, photojournalist and professor at the University of the Arts Londo
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new lawsuits, including man who was 16 at the time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Petitions for union representation doubled under Biden's presidency, first increase since 1970s
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There has been a doubling of petitions by workers to have union representation during President Joe Biden's administration, according to figures released Tuesday by the National Labor Relations Board.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delphi murders: State wants to keep widely circulated composite sketches out of trial
item.description: CARROLL COUNTY, Ind. – The state doesn’t want a pair of composite sketches that were highly publicized early in the investigation to be part of the Delphi murders trial.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, WGN 9

item.title: Which are the most popular Halloween candies in Utah?
item.description: Are you hoping the neighborhood mischief-makers won’t “T.P.” your house on Halloween? Then maybe you should hand out the candies they actually like.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. Robert Roberson, 57, is scheduled to receive a lethal injection on Thursday for the 2002 killing of his 2-year-old daughter, Nikki Curtis.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Members of the Kennedy family gather for funeral of Ethel Kennedy
item.description: CENTERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — Members of the Kennedy family gathered Monday for the funeral of Ethel Kennedy, the wife of the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Middle East latest: US says it will send THAAD defense battery to Israel, along with US troops
item.description: The U.S. is sending a Terminal High Altitude Area Defense battery to Israel, along with US troops to operate it. The Pentagon made the announcement just hours after Iran warned the U.S. to keep its military forces out of Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Misinformation generally has been an increasing concern, most recently with how the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has been responding to the aftermath of Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4
